content.text:
Every person belongs to a family! Families come in many shapes and sizes, but they all have something special in common — the people in them care for each other.

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Types of Families:
• Some families have a mom, dad, and children
• Some families have one parent and children
• Some families include grandparents living together
• Some families have two moms or two dads
• Some children are raised by grandparents, aunts, uncles, or foster families

All of these are real families!

🏠 Family Roles:
In a family, everyone has a role — things they do to help the family:
• Parents and guardians work to earn money and take care of the home
• Children go to school and help with small chores
• Grandparents share wisdom and family stories
• Older siblings help take care of younger ones

🌍 Families Around the World:
Families all over the world eat together, celebrate special days, and pass on traditions. Different cultures have different foods, languages, clothing, and holidays — but every family teaches children about kindness, respect, and belonging.

💛 What makes a family strong?
• Caring for each other
• Communicating and solving problems together
• Spending time together
• Supporting each other through hard times

You are an important part of your family!

diagrams.alt: Illustrations of different types of families from around the world

readingPassage.title: Kai's Special Family
readingPassage.text:
Kai had never thought much about what made a family — until his teacher asked the class to draw their families.

Kai drew himself, his grandmother Mele, his grandfather Joe, and their dog Biscuit. His parents lived far away for work, so Kai lived with his grandparents.

His classmate Sofia drew her mom, her stepdad, her baby sister, and her grandmother who lived next door.

Another classmate, Marcus, drew two dads and himself — plus a cat named Shadow.

When everyone held up their drawings, Kai looked around. All the pictures looked different. Some had lots of people. Some had just a few. Some included pets.

"Are all these really families?" Kai asked.

His teacher, Ms. Chen, smiled. "Yes, absolutely. A family is a group of people — or people and pets — who love each other, take care of each other, and face life together. The shape doesn't matter."

Kai looked at his drawing again. Grandmother Mele made the best soup when he was sick. Grandfather Joe helped him with his reading every night. Biscuit slept at the foot of his bed.

"I have a really great family," Kai said.

Ms. Chen nodded. "You all do."

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Read the second paragraph where Kai describes his drawing.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: According to Ms. Chen, what makes something a family?

options.text: People who love and take care of each other

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Find Ms. Chen's answer when Kai asks if all the drawings are families.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: What does Grandfather Joe do to help Kai?

options.text: Makes soup when he is sick
Helps him with his reading every night

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Read near the end of the story where Kai thinks about what his grandparents do.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: Why did all the family drawings look different?

options.text: The students were not good at drawing
Families come in many different shapes and sizes
Only some students had real families
The teacher gave everyone different instructions

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Think about the main idea of this story and lesson — what is true about all families?

questions.prompt: Which of these is TRUE about families?

options.text: All families must have exactly two parents
Families come in many shapes and sizes
Only families with children are real families
All families live in the same type of home
A game family members play together
Something a person does to help the family
The oldest person in a family

questions.prompt: Which of these makes a family strong?

options.text: Caring for and supporting each other

questions.prompt: Families around the world are similar because they:

options.text: All teach children kindness, respect, and belonging
Have the same number of children

questions.prompt: What role do grandparents often have in a family?
